frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Aurat ki asli izzat tab aur badh jaati hai jab woh kisi ghar ki biwi, kisi bachche ki maa banti hai. Career apni jagah hai, lekin family mein uska maqam unhi rishton se aata hai. Mard ko bhi chahiye ke apni biwi ko sahi raasta dikhaye, kyunki aurat ghar aur nasl ki zimmedari se hi mukammal hoti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a woman's worth and respect as primarily coming from being a wife and mother rather than as an individual. It encodes relational framing and conditional respect tied to service within family and male kin structures.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
